post.description: Generate a chat completion response for the given conversation.
Search the web and retrieve relevant web page contents.
Generate embeddings for a list of texts. Use these embeddings for semantic search, clustering, and other machine learning applications.
Generate contextualized embeddings for document chunks. Chunks from the same document share context awareness, improving retrieval quality for document-based applications.

get.description: Retrieve the response for a given asynchronous chat completion request.
Retrieve a list of all asynchronous chat completion requests for a given user.

post.description: Submit an asynchronous chat completion request.
Generate a response for the provided input with optional web search and reasoning.

200.description:
Successful response. Content type depends on `stream` parameter:
- `stream: false` (default): `application/json` with Response
- `stream: true`: `text/event-stream` with SSE events

get.description: List the models available for the Agent API. Returns model identifiers that can be used with the `POST /v1/agent` endpoint. The response follows the OpenAI List Models format for compatibility with third-party tools.

200.description: Successful response with the list of available models.

get.description: Returns your organization's Computer usage analytics as a bucketed time series for a single dataset. Requires an organization analytics API key, generated by an org admin from Settings → Organization → Computer in the Perplexity web app. Only complete UTC grid buckets are returned: start_time snaps down to its bucket, and the in-progress bucket is never included.

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: Window start in unix seconds (UTC), inclusive. Snapped down to the bucket grid, so the first bucket can include usage from before this time.

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: Window end in unix seconds (UTC), exclusive. Defaults to now; future values are capped at now. The window may span at most 90 days.

parameters.name: bucket_width
parameters.description: Bucket size. Buckets align to the UTC grid.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Buckets per page. 1d: default 7, max 31. 1h: default 24, max 168.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response's next_page. Valid only with the same query parameters it was issued for.

parameters.name: user_email
parameters.description: Restrict results to a single member of your organization. Emails that don't match a current member return a generic 400.

200.description: Bucketed usage for the requested window. Buckets without data carry count 0; the analytics store syncs periodically, so a zero in a recent bucket can mean the data hasn't synced yet.

400.description: Invalid parameters (type: bad_request) or semantically invalid request (type: invalid_request) — e.g. start_time after end_time, a window over 90 days, a misused pagination cursor, or a user_email that doesn't match a member of the organization.

401.description: Missing or invalid API key (type: unauthorized).

404.description: The Computer Analytics API is not enabled for this organization (type: feature_disabled).

429.description: Rate limit exceeded (type: request_rate_limit_exceeded). Limits apply per organization across all of its keys.

max_tokens.title: Max Tokens
max_tokens.description: Maximum number of completion tokens to generate

model.title: Model
model.description: Model to use, for example, sonar-pro

stream.title: Stream
stream.description: If true, returns streaming SSE response

stop.title: Stop
stop.description: Stop sequences. Generation stops when one of these strings is produced

temperature.title: Temperature
temperature.description: Controls randomness in the response. Higher values make output more random. Range: 0-2

top_p.title: Top P
top_p.description: Nucleus sampling parameter. Controls diversity via nucleus sampling

response_format.title: Response Format
response_format.description: Optional. Controls the output format. Omit for default text output. Set `type` to `json_schema` for structured output.

messages.title: Messages
messages.description: Array of messages forming the conversation history

search_mode.title: Search Mode
search_mode.description: Source of search results (web, academic, or sec)

return_images.title: Return Images
return_images.description: When true, include image results in the response

return_related_questions.title: Return Related Questions
return_related_questions.description: When true, generates suggested follow-up queries based on the search results

enable_search_classifier.title: Enable Search Classifier
enable_search_classifier.description: When true, uses a classifier to determine if web search is needed for the query

disable_search.title: Disable Search
disable_search.description: When true, disables all web search capabilities. The model responds based solely on its training data

search_domain_filter.title: Search Domain Filter
search_domain_filter.description: Limit search results to specific domains (e.g. github.com, wikipedia.org)

search_language_filter.title: Search Language Filter
search_language_filter.description: Filter results by language using ISO 639-1 codes (e.g. en, fr, de)

search_recency_filter.title: Search Recency Filter
search_recency_filter.description: Filter by publication recency (hour, day, week, month, or year)

search_after_date_filter.title: Search After Date Filter
search_after_date_filter.description: Return results published after this date (MM/DD/YYYY)

search_before_date_filter.title: Search Before Date Filter
search_before_date_filter.description: Return results published before this date (MM/DD/YYYY)

last_updated_before_filter.title: Last Updated Before Filter
last_updated_before_filter.description: Return results last updated before this date (MM/DD/YYYY)

last_updated_after_filter.title: Last Updated After Filter

image_format_filter.description: Filter image results by format (e.g. png, jpg)

image_domain_filter.title: Image Domain Filter
image_domain_filter.description: Limit image results to specific domains

stream_mode.title: Stream Mode
stream_mode.description: Controls the format of streaming events. 'full' suppresses reasoning events and includes metadata inline; 'concise' emits reasoning events separately

reasoning_effort.title: Reasoning Effort
reasoning_effort.description: Controls how much effort the model spends on reasoning

search_context_size.description:
Controls how much content is extracted from result pages.

- `low` — short passages most relevant to the query.
- `medium` — a balanced amount of content per document.
- `high` (default) — detailed content relevant to the query.

Omit when using `max_tokens` or `max_tokens_per_page`.

max_tokens_per_page.description: Maximum webpage content tokens to extract from each result page

max_steps.description:
Maximum number of research loop steps.
If provided, overrides the preset's max_steps value.
Must be >= 1 if specified. Maximum allowed is 10.

model.description:
Model ID in provider/model format (e.g., "openai/gpt-5", "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6").
If models is also provided, models takes precedence.
Required if neither models nor preset is provided.

models.description:
Model fallback chain. Each model is in provider/model format.
Models are tried in order until one succeeds.
Max 5 models allowed. If set, takes precedence over single model field.
The response.model will reflect the model that actually succeeded.

preset.description:
Preset configuration name (e.g., "fast-search", "pro-search", "deep-research").
Pre-configured model with system prompt and search parameters.
Required if model is not provided.

store.description: OpenAI-compatible storage toggle. Accepted for request compatibility; the echoed response always reports `store: true`.

stream.description: If true, returns SSE stream instead of JSON

ResponsesResponse.description: Non-streaming response returned when stream is false

error.description: Error details if the response failed

results.description: Per-person result entries. Shape matches SearchResult (id, url, title, snippet, source, last_updated).

ResponseStreamEvent.description:
SSE stream event. Discriminate by the `type` field:
- `response.created`: Initial response object
- `response.in_progress`: Response processing started
- `response.completed`: Final response with output
- `response.failed`: Error occurred
- `response.output_item.added`: New output item started
- `response.output_item.done`: Output item completed
- `response.output_text.delta`: Streaming text delta
- `response.output_text.done`: Final text content
- `response.reasoning.started`: Reasoning phase started
- `response.reasoning.search_queries`: Search queries issued
- `response.reasoning.search_results`: Search results received
- `response.reasoning.fetch_url_queries`: URL fetch queries issued
- `response.reasoning.fetch_url_results`: URL fetch results received
- `response.reasoning.stopped`: Reasoning phase complete
ResponseStreamEvent.title: ResponseStreamEvent

ResponseCreatedEvent.description:
Response created event (type: "response.created").
Contains the initial response object.

sequence_number.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number for event ordering

ResponseCreatedEvent.title: ResponseCreatedEvent

ResponseInProgressEvent.description:
Response in progress event (type: "response.in_progress").
Emitted when response processing has started.

sequence_number.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number for event ordering

ResponseInProgressEvent.title: ResponseInProgressEvent

ResponseCompletedEvent.description:
Response event
Contains the full or partial response object.

sequence_number.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number for event ordering

ResponseCompletedEvent.title: ResponseCompletedEvent

ResponseFailedEvent.description:
Response failed event (type: "response.failed").
Contains error details when streaming fails.

sequence_number.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number for event ordering

ResponseFailedEvent.title: ResponseFailedEvent

OutputItemAddedEvent.description:
Output item added event (type: "response.output_item.added").
Emitted when a new output item (message or tool call) starts.

sequence_number.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number for event ordering

OutputItemAddedEvent.title: OutputItemAddedEvent

OutputItemDoneEvent.description:
Output item done event (type: "response.output_item.done").
Emitted when an output item (message or tool call) completes.

sequence_number.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number for event ordering

OutputItemDoneEvent.title: OutputItemDoneEvent

TextDeltaEvent.description:
Text delta event (type: "response.output_text.delta").
Contains incremental text content.

sequence_number.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number for event ordering

TextDeltaEvent.title: TextDeltaEvent

TextDoneEvent.description:
Text done event (type: "response.output_text.done").
Contains the final text content.

sequence_number.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number for event ordering

TextDoneEvent.title: TextDoneEvent

ReasoningStartedEvent.description:
Reasoning started event (type: "response.reasoning.started").
Signals the model has started reasoning/searching.

sequence_number.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number for event ordering

ReasoningStartedEvent.title: ReasoningStartedEvent

SearchQueriesEvent.description:
Search queries event (type: "response.reasoning.search_queries").
Contains search queries being executed.

sequence_number.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number for event ordering

SearchQueriesEvent.title: SearchQueriesEvent

SearchResultsEvent.description:
Search results event (type: "response.reasoning.search_results").
Contains search results returned.

sequence_number.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number for event ordering

SearchResultsEvent.title: SearchResultsEvent

FetchUrlQueriesEvent.description:
URL fetch queries event (type: "response.reasoning.fetch_url_queries").
Contains URLs being fetched.

sequence_number.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number for event ordering

FetchUrlQueriesEvent.title: FetchUrlQueriesEvent

FetchUrlResultsEvent.description:
URL fetch results event (type: "response.reasoning.fetch_url_results").
Contains fetched URL contents.

sequence_number.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number for event ordering

FetchUrlResultsEvent.title: FetchUrlResultsEvent

ReasoningStoppedEvent.description:
Reasoning stopped event (type: "response.reasoning.stopped").
Signals the model has finished reasoning/searching.

sequence_number.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number for event ordering

ReasoningStoppedEvent.title: ReasoningStoppedEvent

input.title: Input
input.description: Input text to embed, encoded as a string or array of strings. Maximum 512 texts per request. Each input must not exceed 32K tokens. All inputs in a single request must not exceed 120,000 tokens combined. Empty strings are not allowed.

dimensions.title: Dimensions
dimensions.description: Number of dimensions for output embeddings (Matryoshka). Range: 128-1024 for pplx-embed-v1-0.6b, 128-2560 for pplx-embed-v1-4b. Defaults to full dimensions (1024 or 2560).

encoding_format.title: Encoding Format
encoding_format.description: Output encoding format for embeddings. base64_int8 returns base64-encoded signed int8 values. base64_binary returns base64-encoded packed binary (1 bit per dimension).

model.title: Model
model.description: The model used to generate embeddings

index.title: Index
index.description: The index of the input text this embedding corresponds to

embedding.title: Embedding
embedding.description: Base64-encoded embedding vector. For base64_int8: decode to signed int8 array (length = dimensions). For base64_binary: decode to packed bits (length = dimensions / 8 bytes).

EmbeddingsUsage.title: Embeddings Usage
EmbeddingsUsage.description: Token usage for the embeddings request

prompt_tokens.title: Prompt Tokens
prompt_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the input texts

input_cost.title: Input Cost
input_cost.description: Cost for input tokens in USD

total_cost.title: Total Cost
total_cost.description: Total cost for the request in USD

ContextualizedEmbeddingsRequest.title: Contextualized Embeddings Request
ContextualizedEmbeddingsRequest.description: Request body for creating contextualized embeddings

input.title: Input
input.description: Nested array structure where each inner array contains chunks from a single document. Chunks within the same document are encoded with document-level context awareness. Maximum 512 documents. Total chunks across all documents must not exceed 16,000. Total tokens per document must not exceed 32K. All chunks in a single request must not exceed 120,000 tokens combined. Empty strings are not allowed.

model.title: Model
model.description: The contextualized embedding model to use

dimensions.title: Dimensions
dimensions.description: Number of dimensions for output embeddings (Matryoshka). Range: 128-1024 for pplx-embed-context-v1-0.6b, 128-2560 for pplx-embed-context-v1-4b. Defaults to full dimensions (1024 or 2560).

encoding_format.title: Encoding Format
encoding_format.description: Output encoding format for embeddings. base64_int8 returns base64-encoded signed int8 values. base64_binary returns base64-encoded packed binary (1 bit per dimension).

ContextualizedEmbeddingsResponse.title: Contextualized Embeddings Response
ContextualizedEmbeddingsResponse.description: Response body for contextualized embeddings request

model.title: Model
model.description: The model used to generate embeddings

index.title: Index
index.description: The index of the document this chunk belongs to

data.title: Data
data.description: List of embedding objects for chunks in this document

Model.description: Represents a model available through the Agent API.

id.description: The model identifier in `provider/model-name` format. Use this value as the `model` parameter in Agent API requests.

object.description: The object type, which is always `model`.

created.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) when the model was created.

owned_by.description: The provider that owns the model (e.g. `openai`, `anthropic`, `google`, `perplexity`, `xai`, `z-ai`, `moonshotai`, `nvidia`).

ListModelsResponse.description: Response from the List Models endpoint. Contains a list of available models.

object.description: The object type, which is always `list`.

ComputerUsageCategoryCount.description: A bucket's count for one value of a category breakdown — e.g. a connector name for connectors, a duration band for task_durations, or paid/promo for credit_usage's Credit Source axis.

start_time.description: Bucket start in unix seconds (UTC), inclusive.

end_time.description: Bucket end in unix seconds (UTC), exclusive.

count.description: Bucket total. For credit_usage this may exceed the sum of any single breakdown — usage without a category attribution counts toward the total but not the breakdown.

by_categories.description: Category breakdowns for this bucket, keyed by the labels declared in the response's top-level `categories` list. An empty list means the bucket has no data for that breakdown.

categories.description: Category breakdown labels the requested dataset surfaces, in canonical render order. Each label is a key in every bucket's `by_categories`.

data.description: Every bucket in the page's window, in chronological order. Buckets without data carry count 0 and an empty breakdown.

has_more.description: Whether more buckets exist beyond this page. False also when the window's remaining buckets are past the current data frontier.

next_page.description: Cursor for the next page; pass as the page parameter with otherwise identical query parameters.

McpToolDef.description: One tool discovered on a remote MCP server.

input_schema.description: The server's JSON Schema for the tool, passed through unmodified.

McpToolDef.title: McpToolDef

McpListToolsOutputItem.description: Tools discovered on one external MCP server at boot. Matches OpenAI's mcp_list_tools item.

error.description: Null when the server's tools were listed successfully.

McpListToolsOutputItem.title: McpListToolsOutputItem

McpCallOutputItem.description: One tool call executed against an external MCP server, modeled on OpenAI's mcp_call item.

output.description: Tool output text; empty when the call failed.

error.description: The failure string when the call failed (also returned to the model in-band); null on success, matching OpenAI's mcp_call.

McpCallOutputItem.title: McpCallOutputItem

McpTool.description: Connects a user-supplied remote MCP server. Agent API discovers the server's tools when the request starts and calls them like native tools. Matches OpenAI's mcp tool. `require_approval`, `connector_id`, and `defer_loading` are not supported and are ignored if sent: every call auto-runs, and only bring-your-own `server_url` is honored.

server_label.description: Unique per request, ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,64}$. Namespaces the server's tools.

server_url.description: HTTPS URL of the remote MCP server. Must be a Streamable HTTP MCP endpoint; the legacy SSE transport is not supported.

authorization.description: An access token passed to the remote MCP server for authentication. Provide the raw token value. Never logged or echoed.

allowed_tools.description: Optional allowlist of tool names. Empty exposes all discovered tools.

McpTool.title: McpTool
